concerns.text: Plan references 'descriptors.csv' but data-model.md defines 'Descriptor Vector' entity. Use consistent terminology across documents (e.g., 'Descriptor Vector' or reference data-model entity name explicitly).

Plan mentions 'contract tests against YAML schemas' but does not explicitly map which scripts validate which schemas (e.g., compute.py → alloy_composition.schema.yaml). Add explicit mapping for traceability.

Principle IV (Single Source of Truth) claims PASS but plan does not explicitly designate which data file is the SSoT for each analysis type. Constitution requires 'Every figure/statistic traces to exactly one row in data/ and one block in code/' - add explicit SSoT designation per analysis.

Principle II (Verified Accuracy) claims PASS but research.md explicitly states no verified sources found for Materials Project/NIST Alloy Database. Clarify that PASS is aspirational (intended compliance) and document the dataset gap as a known limitation affecting production accuracy verification.

Principle V (Versioning Discipline) states 'Content hashes for all artifacts' but data-model.md only shows checksums for data files. Clarify that ALL artifacts (code, models, results, data) carry content hashes per constitution.
